Bowel-prep cheat sheet · Pediatric Gastroenterology
Internal staff reference · Not for patient distribution

BIG PREP — MiraLAX standard · per weight band

{{STANDARD_BAND_ROWS}}
Band Dulcolax (bed+day) Tot. mg MiraLAX caps Tot. g Gatorade Cup Start

Infants <15 kg — two options (clinician choice)

{{INFANT_SUB_ROWS}}
Sub-band MiraLAX caps Tot. g Mix in (Pedialyte or clear apple juice)

MiraLAX schedule: 1 dose/day × 2 days (days −3 and −2). Patient handouts: u15kgPEG / u15kgEnema.

Contingency rescue plan if stools not clear/pale yellow after {{CONTINGENCY_TRIGGER_HOURS}} h

{{CONTINGENCY_ROWS}}
Band Evening rescue Morning rescue
finish ≥ NPO before procedure		 Hard stop
incl. BIG PREP		 If still not clear

Trigger: {{CONTINGENCY_TRIGGER_HOURS}} h after starting BIG PREP without clear/pale-yellow watery stool. NPO clears: {{NPO_SCC_HOURS}} h SCC / {{NPO_PMCH_HOURS}} h PMCH — morning rescue must finish ≥ that window before procedure time. If still not clear after rescue: call office {{OFFICE_PHONE}}. Infants <15 kg: not applicable — see <15 kg block.

Pre-cleanout (suspected constipation) disimpaction + maintenance

{{PRECLEANOUT_ROWS}}
Band Disimpaction Start Daily maintenance

Locations

{{LOC_SCC_NAME}}{{LOC_PMCH_NAME}}
Address{{LOC_SCC_ADDRESS}}{{LOC_PMCH_ADDRESS}}
Facility phone{{LOC_SCC_PHONE}}{{LOC_PMCH_PHONE}}
NPO clears{{NPO_SCC_HOURS}} h before procedure{{NPO_PMCH_HOURS}} h before procedure
Arrival{{LOC_SCC_ARRIVAL_MIN}} min before{{LOC_PMCH_ARRIVAL_MIN}} min before
Mobile sites{{LOC_SCC_MOBILE_LINES}}{{LOC_PMCH_MOBILE_LINES}}

When to call — post-procedure red flags office {{OFFICE_PHONE}}

  • Fever >101°F (38.3°C)
  • Stool that is black or has more than a small streak of blood
  • Severe or worsening abdominal pain, or hard / swollen belly
  • Persistent vomiting, or refusal to drink
  • Difficulty breathing or chest pain — emergency, call 911

Small blood with the first stool can be normal (especially if biopsies taken). Adult must stay 24 h after anesthesia. Most kids back to normal next day.

Medications to hold (7 days before)

  • Iron supplements — all bands
  • Anti-diarrhea medicine — all bands
  • GLP-1 agonists (Ozempic, Wegovy, Mounjaro) — {{GLP1_HOLD_BANDS}} only

Unusual or unlisted meds: ask the family to prepare a list and call the office.

Alternative protocols
CLENPIQ + SUPREP + Lactulose · for MiraLAX intolerance or physician preference

BIG PREP — CLENPIQ {{CLENPIQ_ELIGIBILITY}} · {{CLENPIQ_BOTTLES}} bottles

{{CLENPIQ_ROWS}}
Dose Bottle Window Clears after Cup

No mixing · no Dulcolax · no pre-cleanout. Bottles ready-to-drink (pack ships as 2). Morning-dose clears must finish ≥ SCC {{NPO_SCC_HOURS}} h / PMCH {{NPO_PMCH_HOURS}} h before procedure. GLP-1 hold applies (eligible band includes adolescents). Hidden patient sites: prepclenpiq{,86}.giready.com · egdcolonclenpiq{,86}.giready.com.

BIG PREP — SUPREP {{SUPREP_ELIGIBILITY}} · FDA age {{SUPREP_AGE_FLOOR}}+ · Rx

{{SUPREP_ROWS}}
Dose Solution (mixed) Window Water chasers Cup

Must be diluted in mixing container (do NOT drink straight). No Dulcolax · no pre-cleanout (sulfate stimulant built in; concurrent stimulants forbidden by label). All solution + water finish ≥ SCC {{NPO_SCC_HOURS}} h / PMCH {{NPO_PMCH_HOURS}} h before procedure. Drug-interaction window: tetracyclines / fluoroquinolones / iron / digoxin / chlorpromazine / penicillamine — 2 h before + 6 h after each dose. All other oral meds ≥ 1 h before. GLP-1 hold applies. Hidden patient sites: prepsuprep{,86}.giready.com · egdcolonsuprep{,86}.giready.com.

BIG PREP — Lactulose for MiraLAX-intolerant

{{LACTULOSE_STANDARD_ROWS}}
Band Dulcolax (bed+day) Lactulose Gatorade Cup Start

Infants <15 kg — daily lactulose × 3 days (no big-prep day, no Dulcolax)

{{LACTULOSE_INFANT_ROWS}}
Sub-band Daily lactulose dose Mix in (juice or Pedialyte)

{{LACTULOSE_FOOTNOTE}} Hidden patient sites: preplact{,86}.giready.com.
